claim_bullets.bullet_text: When you take the rest of an array with destructuring ( [first, ...rest] ), you are given a copy of the elements of the array. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.158-159)
The consequence of this is that if you have an array, and you take it's 'rest,' your 'child' array is a copy of the elements of the parent array. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.158-159)
When you take the rest of a linked list with its reference, you are given the exact same nodes of the elements of the original list. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.158-159)

source_record_text: Copy‑on‑write concepts for arrays and linked lists in JavaScript.
